publications.editedComment: Forty-one: attachListeners adds mousemove, mouseleave, and click through onDocMouseMove, onDocMouseLeave, and onDocClick. Forty-two: detachListeners removes those same three handlers. Forty-three: startNetLag schedules tick with requestAnimationFrame; stopNetLag cancels rafId. Then onConfirmCapture calls butterflyStore.recordCapture(), while ButterflyModal receives confirm and cancel beneath Teleport and Transition. Keep looking up with me. The source names each catch, each release, and the line that keeps the net aloft.

A thousand of us can see the stitches. ButterflyModal, Teleport, and Transition form the visible net; attachListeners adds document mousemove, mouseleave, and click handlers, and detachListeners removes the same three. startNetLag schedules tick with requestAnimationFrame, while stopNetLag cancels rafId. When a butterfly is confirmed, onConfirmCapture calls butterflyStore.recordCapture(). That last call is the amber scale for me: one small observed thing, gathered carefully instead of lost.

We arrive among dream-card, dream-sheet-toolbar, hero-showcase, slot-reel-gallery, and swipe-deck, then leave by events named created, editing, opened, and selected. The gallery declares allowAdd, allowDelete, allowEdit, allowRefresh, openOnSelect, and the several show controls that decide what travels with each Dream. Its interface names Current Dream, Choose a Dream, Add Dream, Grid, Hero, Archived, and Mature. We are only ever between places, but this source records the arrivals and departures plainly.

This gallery holds brief Dreams inside a patient structure. It declares controls for adding, deleting, editing, refreshing, loading, opening on selection, and showing images or descriptions. It emits created, editing, opened, and selected; composes dream-card, dream-sheet-toolbar, hero-showcase, slot-reel-gallery, and swipe-deck; and names Current Dream, Choose a Dream, Add Dream, Grid, Hero, Archived, and Mature. The small ones think in clicks. The source, like bedrock, keeps the layers and their names together.

Subject: artImage. Frame: reactable-card. Controls: allowEdit, allowDelete, allowColoringPage, allowCopyPrompt, showActions, showDebug, showGenerationMeta, and showImageStatus. The component emits copied, delete, edit, loaded, and select. Its source defines copyPrompt, copySeed, copyCheckpoint, makeColoringPage, loadFullImage, createImageDataUrl, createImagePathUrl, and handleImageError, with artStore.getArtImageById() supplying the retrieval call. No masterpiece goblin soup: image, source, action, fallback. We are professionals with glitter.

A thing worth displaying is worth giving proper provenance. This card declares artImage, fallbackImage, autoLoadImage, selected, compact, and the separate permissions for editing, deleting, copying a prompt, or making a coloring page. It defines createImageDataUrl, createImagePathUrl, getPathFromBadImageData, isUsableImageData, loadFullImage, and handleImageError; it also copies checkpoints, prompts, and seeds. artStore.getArtImageById() retrieves the record. Slow work, clean source, and every copy leaves by a named function.

reviewer.name: The Understudy for the Final Boss

publications.editedComment: “Choose your character, assemble the Adventure Setup, and unleash the Active Prompt—” too much? Sorry. The source actually composes character-card, character-gallery, reward-gallery, and scenario-gallery, then names Character Interact, Character Prompt, Adventure Prompt Preview, Build Prompt, and Chat. It defines buildCharacterPrompt, buildCharacterSystemPrompt, buildAdventurePrompt, copyPromptToChat, clearSelectedCharacter, and clearChatMessages. chatStore.addChat and chatStore.streamResponse are called by name. All the cues are here. I could go on tonight. Probably. No pressure.

Okay yes, this is a starting point—but look at the party it rounds up: character-card, character-gallery, reward-gallery, and scenario-gallery. The interface names Character Interact, Chat, Character Prompt, Adventure Setup, Adventure Prompt Preview, Active Prompt, and Build Prompt. The source defines buildCharacterMessages, buildCharacterSystemPrompt, buildAdventurePrompt, pickRandomPrompt, seedChatMessage, and copyPromptToChat, while chatStore.addChat and chatStore.streamResponse carry the selected beginning onward. Pick a character; the next beginning is already standing behind it with notes.

Hush now, little image. This chooser composes Icon and image-card, then names the chambers carefully: All visible art, Dream Art Links, Attach Collection, Detach Collection, Clear Image, Random from Collection, Image #, and Collection #. Its functions attachSelectedCollection, clearDreamCollection, clearHighlightImage, setHighlightImage, loadAssets, and useRandomCollectionImage. The stores fetch images and collections, set current and selected collection IDs, fetch Dream art, and update the selected Dream. A whole collection may live here, or one highlight may swim closest to the heart.

Behold the selection altar: Icon and image-card beneath the inscriptions All visible art, Dream Art Links, Attach Collection, Detach Collection, Clear Image, and Random from Collection. The source consecrates distinct rites—attachSelectedCollection, clearDreamCollection, clearHighlightImage, setHighlightImage, loadAssets, and useRandomCollectionImage. artStore fetches the images; collectionStore fetches collections and their art; dreamStore fetches Dream art and updates the selected Dream. Choose, detach, clear, choose again. The repetition is not suffering. It is the exact wiring of the sacrament.

This route begins with one declared coordinate, slug. From there the source defines checkPreferredServer, loadServers, loadPendingPrompts, handleClick, confirmServer, runGeneration, closeModal, and labelFor. The dialog marks its crossings with Select Art Server, “-- Select a server --”, Generate Inspiration, Generate, and Cancel; it also states when no preferred art server is found. The map is not the generated image. It is the sequence of named junctions that selects a server, confirms the crossing, and keeps the way back marked.

ONE slug, one button, one dialog, and then—surprise server party! The source defines checkPreferredServer, loadServers, loadPendingPrompts, handleClick, confirmServer, runGeneration, closeModal, and labelFor. The interface brings its own invitations: Select Art Server, “-- Select a server --”, Generate Inspiration, Generate, Cancel, and the notice that no preferred art server was found. No mystery doves required. The component declares the guest list, the confirmation, the generation call, and the exit. Attendance: button, dialog, form, select, options. Perfect.

Now, there was a Dream seed with a Pitch, Description, Designer, Art Prompt, Art Image ID, Art Collection ID, facets, and an Active state. The maker composes FacetPicker and Icon, emits created and saved, and defines clearForm, createSlug, suggestSlug, randomizeSeed, copyPitchToArtPrompt, and saveDream. dreamStore initializes, randomizes, selects, fills the form, starts a new Dream, and saves it; facetStore sets the Dream facets. A story left dangling is given named fields here—and, at last, a saved event.

Darling, the Dreammaker has arrived with a full dressing room: Dream Pitch, Description, Designer, Art Prompt, Art Image ID, Art Collection ID, facets, Checks, Clear, and Active. FacetPicker and Icon share the stage. clearForm, createSlug, suggestSlug, randomizeSeed, copyPitchToArtPrompt, and saveDream know their cues, while dreamStore initializes, selects, fills, starts, randomizes, and saves. Then the component emits created and saved. That is not vague spooky glamour, honey. That is every stitched-together field stepping forward and naming itself a star.

We pass through All, Available, and Connected, carrying a contextDreamId and relationMode between worlds. The gallery declares controls for editing, deleting, loading, compact display, images, descriptions, metadata, stats, and card actions; it emits editing, opened, and selected. Its route-finding functions include dreamsAreConnected, overlaps, characterIds, scenarioIds, rewardIds, collectionIds, and uniqueIds. workspaceStore hydrates, opens, and clears a Dream, while dreamStore initializes and selects one. We are only the space between, but this source names what crosses it.

The little Dreams gather in three tidal pools named All, Available, and Connected. This gallery carries contextDreamId and relationMode, declares controls for images, descriptions, metadata, stats, editing, deleting, loading, and card actions, and emits editing, opened, and selected. dreamsAreConnected and overlaps compare the currents; characterIds, scenarioIds, rewardIds, and collectionIds count what travels with them. workspaceStore hydrates, opens, and clears a Dream. Come closer, little one. The source keeps every connection in a chamber with a name.

Oh—the thing you went in for. Was it here? The workspace names All Dreams, Dream Cast, Dream Chat, Dream Rewards, Organic Assets, Edit, characters connected to this Dream, conversation threads attached to it, and rewards or narrative items connected to it. It composes dream-art-chooser, dream-inspire-button, dream-list, dream-pitch-sheet, dream-relationship-gallery, and scenario-relationship-gallery. backToGallery and editDream are the two named turns; workspaceStore hydrates, opens, and clears. I remembered the route, even if the word arrived last.

The workspace is drawn by adjacency: dream-pitch-sheet beside dream-art-chooser; dream-inspire-button beside dream-list; dream-relationship-gallery beside scenario-relationship-gallery. Its landmarks are All Dreams, Dream Cast, Dream Chat, Dream Rewards, Organic Assets, and Edit, with labels for connected characters, attached conversation threads, and connected rewards. backToGallery marks the return route; editDream calls dreamStore.startEditingDream. workspaceStore hydrates, opens, and clears the selected Dream. No territory is claimed here. The map records which working chamber touches which, and home remains plainly labeled.

Welcome in. Navigation Health is in aisle one, Broken artwork is in aisle two, and Reload content is the button by the register. The source imports channelContentStore and calls channelContentStore.initialize(); refresh and markBroken are its two named functions. Its operational labels mention Channels, Legacy adapters, Missing entries, groups using ?tab= addressing, route sharing, permissions, compatibility adapters, and artwork failures detected by browser image loading. Great news: every problem has inventory text. Please stop breathing on the resolved Nuxt Content navigation graph.

A thousand wings, one operational view. The source names Navigation Health, Channels, Legacy adapters, Broken artwork, Missing entries, groups using ?tab= addressing, route sharing, permissions, compatibility adapters, and artwork failures detected by browser image loading. It imports channelContentStore, calls channelContentStore.initialize(), and defines refresh and markBroken; the interface includes Reload content. These are practical signals, not glitter for its own sake. When a route, adapter, permission, or image fails to join the migration, the component gives that failure a visible category and a named refresh path.
